Elements.Text: In this updated example, the Switch class now relies on the Switchable interface instead of the specific LightBulb class. This follows the Dependency Inversion Principle, which makes it easier to swap devices without altering the Switch class.

By using this principle, you can easily add new devices (like a fan or a TV) without needing to change the Switch class. This approach reduces the interdependence between classes, making the code more flexible and adaptable.
